title: Valerianoides mutabilis
intro: Valerianoides mutabilis, also known as the "changeable valerian," is a flowering plant species in the family Apiaceae, characterized by its unique, changeable flower color and medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and pain. The bioactive constituents of Valerianoides mutabilis include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Valerianoides mutabilis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including stress, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Valerianoides mutabilis is classified as a species within the genus Valerianoides, a type of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae. The family Valerianaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the valerian family or valerianwort family. The order of this plant is Dipsacales and the class is Magnoliopsida. Further classification places Valerianoides mutabilis within the subclass Asteridae, the division Magnoliophyta, and the kingdom Plantae. The Valerianoides mutabilis classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
